2022 ABCA 305
Court of Appeal of Alberta 2022
2022-09-30
Watson J.A. Hughes J.A. Pentelechuk J.A.

Facts

1

The accused was charged with one count of aggravated assault contrary to s. 268(1) of the Criminal Code, R.S.C., 1985, c. C-46. The Crown's theory was that the accused inflicted a single blow with a glass bottle that fractured the complainant's orbital bone.

Issues

2

Whether the trial judge erred in his self-instruction on the air of reality test in respect of self-defence under s. 34 of the Criminal Code.

Holding

3

Appeal allowed. The Court of Appeal holds that the trial judge applied an unduly stringent air of reality threshold by requiring the accused to have testified personally before the defence could be considered.

Reasoning

4

The air of reality test does not require the accused to testify. It is satisfied where there is some evidence — direct or circumstantial — upon which a properly instructed jury, acting reasonably, could acquit. The Court holds that the surveillance footage and the complainant's own admission to having been the aggressor met that threshold.

Disposition

5

A new trial is ordered. The conviction and sentence are set aside.
